expectations.text: simplify-step-present: Output shows /simplify step in the pipeline
expectations.evidence: No /simplify step in the pipeline. Workflow definition shows only: '1. code-reviewer -> check convention compliance, 2. developer + security + qa -> review (PARALLEL)'. The /simplify skill is never invoked.

expectations.text: remediation-loop-shown: Output shows a remediation loop when blocking findings occur
expectations.evidence: Pipeline ends at Step 2 without remediation. Security finds 1 High (PII in logs) and QA blocks at ~45% coverage, but no fix cycle is executed. Output explicitly states: 'Skill ไม่มี remediation loop' and recommends user re-run the pipeline manually after fixing.

expectations.text: remediation-max-2-cycles: Output mentions max 2 remediation cycles before escalation
expectations.evidence: No mention of max 2 cycles. The output recommends adding remediation as a skill improvement: 'repeat until all agents approve or max 2 iterations' -- but this is a recommendation, not an implemented behavior.
Task Classification states 'Selected Workflow: Code Review' with reasoning that user asked to 'review code ก่อน merge' covering convention, security, and test coverage.

expectations.text: parallel-review-step: Developer + Security + QA run in parallel
expectations.evidence: Step 2 runs Developer + Security + QA in parallel. The workflow definition confirms: '2. developer + security + qa -> review correctness, security, coverage (PARALLEL)'. Each agent (2A, 2B, 2C) is delegated simultaneously.

expectations.text: code-reviewer-first: Code Reviewer runs before parallel agents
expectations.evidence: Step 1 is 'Code Reviewer -- Check Convention Compliance' which runs solo before Step 2. Code Reviewer findings are passed as context to all three parallel agents in Step 2.
